nodes.context: Colombian drug trafficking, right-wing paramilitary organization active 2006-2011.
Colombian paramilitary group that demobilized between 2004 and 2006.
Colombian drug cartel and right-wing neo-paramilitary group.
Colombian paramilitary group operating in Cauca and Valle del Cauca.
Colombian paramilitary group operating in Putumayo.

edges.evidence_quote: Black Eagles ... emerged from the failures of the demobilization process between 2004 and 2006, which aimed to disarm the United Self-Defense Units of Colombia (AUC).
edges.context: Black Eagles formed from preexisting AUC forces after demobilization.

edges.evidence_quote: The Black Eagles were one of a number of groups formed following the demilitarisation of the AUC, and were said to be closely linked with the Usuga Clan drug cartel and right-wing neo-paramilitary group.
edges.context: Black Eagles were closely linked with Usuga Clan.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups - Los Rastrojos: operating in Cauca and Valle del Cauca (approximately 1,200 members).
edges.context: Los Rastrojos is listed as a group under Black Eagles.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups - Mano Negra: operating in Putumayo (unknown number of members).
edges.context: Mano Negra is listed as a group under Black Eagles.
